expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: modern grid hyperlink-style coverage separates contextual-link styling from ordinary element rendering
expectations.evidence: result.md explicitly separates linked vs non-linked rendering and verifies no bleed: “Contextual-link styling is visually distinct from ordinary cell text”, “Identify a baseline cell that has no contextual link… Observe the baseline cell text styling”, “Identify a cell that has a contextual link… The linked object is visually distinguishable… An indicator icon is present…”, plus “Mixed cells: only linked objects get hyperlink styling (no ‘style bleed’)” and “Same cell, different objects: only the object(s) with contextual links are styled.” execution_notes.md also states: “Scenarios explicitly cover the case focus: hyperlink-style coverage separates contextual-link styling from ordinary element rendering.”

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase4a
expectations.evidence: execution_notes.md declares “Phase alignment (phase4a) - Output is a subcategory-only QA draft (no canonical top-layer categories…)”. result.md header: “Phase 4a (BCIN-7547)”, and the content is scenario-level QA coverage without higher-level category framing, consistent with phase4a-contract guidance.
